hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, multi-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the search space of hypothetical linear polyamines, then it will accelerate the discovery of high-performance CO2 capture materials by efficiently identifying candidates with superior adsorption capacity compared to known reference polyamines within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate model-guided search exploits structure-property relationships encoded in pre-trained embeddings to prioritize promising regions of chemical space without exhaustive enumeration.
